Ms. Dawn Wiser 
State Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804 
Dear Ms. Wiser: 
September 15, 1977 
Re: 
Excess Campaign Funds 
I have been asked a question upon which I 
would appreciate an informal opinion of the FPPC Staff. 
A Councilman was elected in the City's Councilmatic 
elections of last May. 
He has campaign funds left over. 
I don't know the exact amount but it probably is in the 
range of $1,000 to $2,000. 
He asks if he may donate these 
funds to charity. 
Specifically, he wishes to make a 
contribution to a local church. 
The money is to be used 
for a specific charitable program of the church and not 
general church purposes. 
May he do so? 
My question presumes 
that he would subsequently be required to report the use 
of these funds as presently required. 
I also understand that the Commission has made 
some ruling on the distribution of excess campaign funds 
back to the donors. 
I would appreciate copies of any 
rulings. 
Thank you for your assistance. 
truly yours, 
~,ff~ 
City Attorney

sections.question: A Councilman was elected in the City's Councilmatic elections of last May. He has campaign funds left over. I don't know the exact amount but it probably is in the range of $1,000 to $2,000. He asks if he may donate these funds to charity. Specifically, he wishes to make a contribution to a local church. The money is to be used for a specific charitable program of the church and not general church purposes. May he do so? My question presumes that he would subsequently be required to report the use of these funds as presently required. I also understand that the Commission has made some ruling on the distribution of excess campaign funds back to the donors. I would appreciate copies of any rulings.
sections.question_synthetic: May a newly elected councilman donate excess campaign funds ($1,000-$2,000) to a local church for a specific charitable program, and what are the reporting requirements and Commission rulings on distribution of excess campaign funds?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The document appears to be an incoming request letter rather than a completed FPPC response, so no conclusion/advice is provided in this excerpt.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter (dated September 15, 1977) and a fragmentary FPPC response letter (dated September 25, 1977). The response portion is severely damaged/illegible, making extraction of actual FPPC conclusion impossible. Classified as advice_letter based on document type indicator, but actual FPPC response content is not recoverable from this document state.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: A Councilman was elected in the City's Councilmatic elections of last May. He has campaign funds left over. I don't know the exact amount but it probably is in the range of $1,000 to $2,000. He asks if he may donate these funds to charity. Specifically, he wishes to make a contribution to a local church. The money is to be used for a specific charitable program of the church and not general church purposes. May he do so? My question presumes that he would subsequently be required to report the use of these funds as presently required. I also understand that the Commission has made some ruling on the distribution of excess campaign funds back to the donors. I would appreciate copies of any rulings.

CONCLUSION: The document appears to be an incoming request letter rather than a completed FPPC response, so no conclusion/advice is provided in this excerpt.

embedding.summary: A city attorney requests an informal FPPC opinion on whether a councilman may donate excess campaign funds to a local church for charitable purposes and asks for copies of Commission rulings on excess campaign fund distribution.
